169: func TestIntegration_ConversationLoop_SingleToolCall(t *testing.T) {
170: 	resetRunCmd(t)
171: 
172: 	mock := testutil.NewMockLLMServer(t, []testutil.MockLLMResponse{
173: 		// Turn 1: parent returns tool_use calling helper-agent
174: 		testutil.AnthropicToolUseResponse("Delegating.", []testutil.MockToolCall{
175: 			{ID: "toolu_1", Name: "call_agent", Input: map[string]string{"agent": "helper-agent", "task": "say hello"}},
176: 		}),
177: 		// Sub-agent call: helper returns a simple response
178: 		testutil.AnthropicResponse("hello from helper"),
179: 		// Turn 2: parent returns final text
180: 		testutil.AnthropicResponse("The helper said: hello from helper"),
181: 	})
182: 
183: 	configDir, _ := testutil.SetupXDGDirs(t)
184: 	writeAgentConfig(t, configDir, "loop-parent", `name = "loop-parent"
185: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
186: sub_agents = ["helper-agent"]
187: `)
188: 	writeAgentConfig(t, configDir, "helper-agent", `name = "helper-agent"
189: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
190: `)
191: 
192: 	t.Setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")
193: 	t.Setenv("AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", mock.URL())
194: 
195: 	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
196: 	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
197: 	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
198: 	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
199: 	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "loop-parent"})
200: 
201: 	err := rootCmd.Execute()
202: 	if err != nil {
203: 		t.Fatalf("expected nil error, got: %v", err)
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	output := buf.String()
207: 	if !strings.Contains(output, "The helper said: hello from helper") {
208: 		t.Errorf("expected stdout to contain final response, got %q", output)
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	if mock.RequestCount() != 3 {
212: 		t.Errorf("expected 3 requests, got %d", mock.RequestCount())
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	// Second request (index 1) is the sub-agent call - should contain the task
216: 	if !strings.Contains(mock.Requests[1].Body, "say hello") {
217: 		t.Errorf("expected sub-agent request to contain task 'say hello', got %q", mock.Requests[1].Body)
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	// Third request (index 2) should contain the tool result from the helper
221: 	if !strings.Contains(mock.Requests[2].Body, "hello from helper") {
222: 		t.Errorf("expected third request to contain tool result, got %q", mock.Requests[2].Body)
223: 	}
224: }

14: // SetupXDGDirs creates an isolated XDG directory structure for testing.
15: // It sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME and XDG_DATA_HOME to temp directories and creates
16: // the axe subdirectory tree (agents/, skills/ under config; axe/ under data).
17: // Returns the axe-level config path and data path.
18: func SetupXDGDirs(t *testing.T) (configDir, dataDir string) {
19: 	t.Helper()
20: 
21: 	root := t.TempDir()
22: 
23: 	configHome := filepath.Join(root, "config")
24: 	dataHome := filepath.Join(root, "data")
25: 
26: 	t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", configHome)
27: 	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", dataHome)
28: 
29: 	configDir = filepath.Join(configHome, "axe")
30: 	dataDir = filepath.Join(dataHome, "axe")
31: 
32: 	dirs := []string{
33: 		filepath.Join(configDir, "agents"),
34: 		filepath.Join(configDir, "skills"),
35: 		dataDir,
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	for _, d := range dirs {
39: 		if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0755); err != nil {
40: 			t.Fatalf("failed to create directory %s: %v", d, err)
41: 		}
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	return configDir, dataDir
45: }

48: // Files are copied byte-for-byte with no parsing or validation.
49: func SeedFixtureAgents(t *testing.T, srcDir, dstAgentsDir string) {
50: 	t.Helper()
51: 
52: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(srcDir)
53: 	if err != nil {

55: 	}

57: 	for _, entry := range entries {

59: 			continue
60: 		}

62: 			continue
63: 		}
64: 
65: 		srcPath := filepath.Join(srcDir, entry.Name())

69: 		}

75: 	}

134: // findModuleRoot walks up from the given directory to find go.mod.
135: func findModuleRoot(start string) (string, error) {
136: 	dir := start
137: 	for {
138: 		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "go.mod")); err == nil {
139: 			return dir, nil
140: 		}
141: 		parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
142: 		if parent == dir {
143: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("could not find module root (go.mod)")
144: 		}
145: 		dir = parent
146: 	}
147: }

149: // BuildBinary compiles the axe binary once per test process and returns the
150: // cached path. Subsequent calls return the same path without rebuilding.
151: // If the build fails, t.Fatal is called with the error.
152: func BuildBinary(t *testing.T) string {
153: 	t.Helper()
154: 
155: 	buildOnce.Do(func() {
156: 		// Find module root by walking up from this file's directory
157: 		_, thisFile, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
158: 		thisDir := filepath.Dir(thisFile)
159: 
160: 		moduleRoot, err := findModuleRoot(thisDir)
161: 		if err != nil {
162: 			buildErr = err
163: 			return
164: 		}
165: 
166: 		// Create temp dir for the binary (NOT t.TempDir - must persist across tests)
167: 		cacheDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "axe-test-bin-*")
168: 		if err != nil {
169: 			buildErr = fmt.Errorf("failed to create cache dir: %w", err)
170: 			return
171: 		}
172: 		buildCacheDir = cacheDir
173: 
174: 		binName := "axe"
175: 		if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
176: 			binName = "axe.exe"
177: 		}
178: 		binPath := filepath.Join(cacheDir, binName)
179: 
180: 		cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, ".")
181: 		cmd.Dir = moduleRoot
182: 		var stderr strings.Builder
183: 		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
184: 
185: 		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
186: 			buildErr = fmt.Errorf("go build failed: %w\nstderr: %s", err, stderr.String())
187: 			return
188: 		}
189: 
190: 		buildBinPath = binPath
191: 	})
192: 
193: 	if buildErr != nil {
194: 		t.Fatalf("BuildBinary: %v", buildErr)
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	return buildBinPath
198: }
199: 
200: // CleanupBinary removes the cached binary directory. Intended to be called
201: // from TestMain after all tests have run.
202: func CleanupBinary() {
203: 	if buildCacheDir != "" {
204: 		_ = os.RemoveAll(buildCacheDir)
205: 	}
206: }
207: 
208: // resetBuildState resets the build state for testing purposes.
209: // This is only used in tests for CleanupBinary itself.
210: func resetBuildState() {
211: 	buildOnce = sync.Once{}
212: 	buildBinPath = ""
213: 	buildErr = nil
214: 	buildCacheDir = ""
215: }

Here are the best existing pieces to build an end-to-end MCP smoke test for `run`:

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go`  
  Contains in-test MCP server builders (`newMCPStreamableServer`, `newMCPComplexSchemaServer`) using `mcp.NewServer` + `httptest`; good reference for spinning up fake MCP servers and tool behaviors.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/mcpclient/router_test.go`  
  Exercises multi-server MCP registration/dispatch/collision flows; useful patterns for validating tool routing across MCP servers.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/testutil/mockserver.go`  
  Reusable mock LLM HTTP server (`NewMockLLMServer`) plus canned response helpers (`AnthropicToolUseResponse`, `OpenAIResponse`, etc.) to drive deterministic `run` conversation loops while MCP tools are invoked.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/cmd/run_integration_test.go`  
  In-process `run` integration harness (rootCmd execution, isolated XDG setup, dynamic agent TOML writing). Good base for adding MCP-enabled run integration tests.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/cmd/smoke_test.go`  
  Binary-level smoke harness (`runAxe`) that executes compiled CLI in a child process with isolated env; best existing true smoke-test scaffolding.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/testutil/testutil.go`  
  Core helpers: `SetupXDGDirs`, `SeedFixtureAgents`, `SeedFixtureSkills`, and `BuildBinary`/`CleanupBinary` for stable integration/smoke setup.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/cmd/run_test.go`  
  Existing `run` MCP coverage for dry-run output (`TestRunCmd_DryRun_WithMCPServers` / `...NoMCPServers`) with inline `[[mcp_servers]]` config.

- `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/cmd/testdata/agents/`  
  Existing agent fixture directory used by smoke/golden tests (no MCP fixture currently).

Notable gap: there is no current `cmd`-level end-to-end test that combines real `run` execution + live fake MCP server + mock LLM in one flow; the above files provide almost all building blocks.
